why.title: E-invoicing is becoming mandatory in the UAE
why.subtitle: The UAE is rolling out mandatory e-invoicing under the Ministry of Finance's Electronic Invoicing System. Businesses must exchange invoices through an Accredited Service Provider (ASP) using the Peppol PINT AE standard.
why.point1Body: Switching ASPs mid-implementation means re-integrating your ERP, retraining your team and re-testing compliance. Getting it right the first time matters.
why.point2Body: Provider pricing ranges from affordable SME bundles to enterprise contracts. The right choice depends on your invoice volume, systems and budget.
why.point3Title: We do the matching for you
why.point3Body: Tell us your requirements and budget. Our team compares the pre-approved providers and connects you with the one that fits — at no cost to you.

directoryPreview.title: The complete pre-approved provider list
directoryPreview.subtitle: Every provider recognised by the UAE Ministry of Finance, kept current.

how.title: How it works
how.step1Body: Share your company size, invoice volume, current software and budget — takes 2 minutes.
how.step2Body: Our team evaluates the pre-approved ASPs against your requirements and price point.
how.step3Body: We introduce you to the best-fit provider. No obligation, no fees for your business.

faqTeaser.title: New to UAE e-invoicing?

quiz.metaTitle: UAE E-Invoicing Readiness Check — Free 2-Minute Assessment
quiz.metaDescription: Is your business ready for mandatory UAE e-invoicing? Take the free 2-minute readiness assessment and get a personalised action plan plus a free provider match.
quiz.title: E-Invoicing Readiness Check
quiz.subtitle: Answer {total} quick questions to see how ready your business is for the UAE e-invoicing mandate — and what to do next.

q1.text: Does your business currently issue invoices electronically (not PDF-by-email)?

q2.text: What accounting or ERP system do you use?
q2.a1: A major system (SAP, Oracle, Dynamics, Zoho, Tally…)

q3.text: Are you registered for VAT / Corporate Tax in the UAE?

q4.text: Have you heard of the Peppol PINT AE invoice format?
q4.a1: Yes, and we know what it requires
q4.a2: Heard of it, but unclear on details

q5.text: Do you have a budget allocated for e-invoicing compliance?

results.title: Your readiness score

high.label: Well prepared
high.body: Your business has strong foundations. Your main task now is choosing the right Accredited Service Provider and completing integration and testing before your go-live date.

medium.label: Getting there
medium.body: You have some building blocks in place, but there are gaps to close — most businesses at this stage benefit from an ASP that offers guided onboarding and ERP integration support.

low.label: Early days
low.body: You're at the start of the journey — and that's fine, but the clock is ticking. The right ASP can handle most of the complexity for you. Start the conversation early.

results.cta: Now tell us your requirements below and we'll match you with the provider that fits your situation and budget — free.
results.trackBody: We saved your details — a specialist will follow up within one business day. Follow the status anytime:

about.metaTitle: About Us — UAE E-Invoicing Providers Directory
about.metaDescription: Who is behind the UAE e-invoicing provider directory, what we do, and how our free provider matching works.
about.title: About this directory
about.body1: We maintain the most complete and current independent directory of e-invoicing service providers pre-approved under the UAE Ministry of Finance's Electronic Invoicing System accreditation framework.
about.body2: Choosing an Accredited Service Provider is one of the most consequential compliance decisions a UAE business will make in the coming years. Pricing models, ERP integrations, onboarding quality and support vary enormously between providers — and comparing them one by one takes weeks.
about.body3: That's why we offer free provider matching: tell us your invoice volume, systems and budget, and our team shortlists the providers that genuinely fit. We're independent, we're not owned by any provider, and using our matching service is free for businesses.
about.howWeKeepCurrent: How we keep the directory current
about.howWeKeepCurrentBody: The directory is reviewed and refreshed continuously against official and public sources, and every entry shows when the directory was last updated. If you spot an inaccuracy, tell us and we'll fix it promptly.

registry.metaTitle: UAE E-Invoicing ASP Registry — All {count} Pre-Approved Providers & Contact Details ({year})
registry.metaDescription: The full registry of all {count} UAE Ministry of Finance pre-approved e-invoicing service providers with official contact persons, emails and phone numbers. Updated {date}.
registry.title: UAE E-Invoicing Service Provider Registry
registry.subtitle: All {count} pre-approved eInvoicing Service Providers with their official contact details, in alphabetical order. Updated {date}.
registry.intro1: Under Article 15 of Ministerial Decision No. 64 of 2025 on the eligibility criteria and accreditation procedure for Service Providers under the Electronic Invoicing System, the UAE Ministry of Finance maintains a list of pre-approved eInvoicing Service Providers. Final accreditation is granted in accordance with Article 16 of the same Decision.
registry.intro2: The registry below mirrors that official list, including each provider's designated contact person(s), email and phone number, and is refreshed regularly — the current version is shown as of the date above. This is an independent directory; always verify a provider's status with the Ministry of Finance before contracting.

matchCta.title: Don't want to call {count} providers one by one?
matchCta.body: Tell us your requirements and budget once — our team shortlists the right providers for you and makes the introduction. Free for businesses.
